summary: This is an extremely brief post that simply links to a Stack Overflow Blog entry about forking in the Unix community. Joel references Freud's concept of 'the narcissism of minor differences' to explain why the Unix world loves to take sides and fork projects. The post is essentially a pointer to content published on the Stack Overflow blog rather than a standalone essay.
key_points: The Unix community has a tendency to fork projects and take sides over minor disagreements
Joel draws a parallel between open source forking culture and Freud's 'narcissism of minor differences'
The post redirects readers to the Stack Overflow blog for the full discussion
money_quotes: The Unix world loves to take sides.
I don't have to blog about this; Freud already did, in 1930. He called it 'the narcissism of minor differences'
key_insight: The tendency of open source communities to fork and factionally divide mirrors what Freud identified as the narcissism of minor differences — fighting hardest over the smallest distinctions.
